metadata.working_title: Ensemble mixture-model refinement of atomic structures in cryo-EM density maps with joint B-factor estimation (TEMPy-REFF)

abstract_sketch.problem: Cryo-EM density maps are routinely interpreted by fitting atomic models, but existing density-guided refinement methods assume a single-structure representation and struggle with maps that have heterogeneous local resolution arising from conformational flexibility, multibody reconstructions, or composite maps.
abstract_sketch.gap: No current method jointly estimates atomic positions and B-factors in a self-consistent probabilistic framework, nor provides a principled procedure for segmenting a map into its constituent chains or composing multiple focused maps without seams or double-counting.
abstract_sketch.approach: TEMPy-REFF formulates each atom as a Gaussian component of a mixture model with a uniform background term, iterating an expectation-maximisation scheme that yields self-consistent estimates of position and B-factor, then generates a structural ensemble by perturbing atoms according to their B-factors and locally minimising with openMM under the AMBER forcefield.
abstract_sketch.key_results: On a benchmark of 366 cryo-EM maps from EMDB spanning 1.8-7.1 Angstrom resolution, TEMPy-REFF reaches ensemble CCC close to 0.9 and yields average CCC improvements of 0.197 over deposited models (p=0.0006) and 0.150 over CERES (p=0.003). B-factors converge within 10-11 iterations and deviate by only 0.87 Angstrom-squared across 366 cases when the initialisation is varied. On RNA polymerase III (EMD-3178, PDB 5FJ8) ensemble CCC rises from 0.82 to 0.94, and AlphaFold2-initialised refinement of SARS-CoV-2 RNA polymerase (EMD-30127) recovers residues absent from the deposited PDB 6M71 model.
abstract_sketch.impact: The mixture-model formulation unifies position refinement, B-factor estimation, ensemble generation, map segmentation, and composite-map construction within one probabilistic framework, enabling automated and reproducible model building for flexible macromolecular assemblies.

sections.title: Introduction
content_bullets: Cryo-EM reaches near-atomic resolution but interpretation requires fitting atomic models with accurate resolution estimates that vary locally
Existing density-guided refinement uses scalar-field gradients, CCC optimisation, or Bayesian expectation-maximisation, with MD, minimisation, normal modes, or Fourier methods for sampling
All existing methods rely on blurring a single model globally or locally; this fails for flexible systems and for composite maps assembled from multibody or focused reconstructions
Population-of-models approaches capture flexibility; mixture modelling offers a principled density representation when iterated to local optimum

We introduce TEMPy-REFF: expectation-maximisation with one Gaussian per atom plus uniform background, jointly estimating positions and B-factors and supporting ensemble, segmentation, and composition tasks

sections.title: Related Work
content_bullets: Density-guided refinement: Phenix real-space refinement, MDFF, Flex-EM, gradient-based and CCC-based optimisation
Sampling strategies: molecular dynamics, minimisation, normal-mode analysis, Fourier-space methods
Ensemble and population-of-models approaches in cryo-EM and NMR; X-ray crystallography multi-conformer refinement
Gaussian mixture model representations of maps (coarse-grained) and EM-Fit style Bayesian fitting
Local quality scores: SMOCf, Q-score, EMRinger, CaBLAM, and local FSC
Composite and focused maps: current ad hoc addition or averaging procedures
Protein structure prediction: AlphaFold2 and RoseTTAFold as sources of initial models

subsections.title: Mixture-model formulation of the cryo-EM map
content_bullets: Each atom a 3D Gaussian with atomic-number weighting, scalar B-factor as variance
Uniform background distribution with scalar kbg parameter
Responsibility G_i / M_S as soft-assignment

subsections.title: Expectation-maximisation updates
content_bullets: Map-weighted position update as intensity-weighted centre of mass over voxels
B-factor update as variance of weighted distribution
Uniform background re-estimation from residual intensity
Occupancies fixed to 1 to reduce degrees of freedom

subsections.title: Forcefield coupling and MD/minimisation
content_bullets: openMM with AMBER and GB-Neck2 implicit solvent (kappa=3)
Fictitious map-derived force F_map = K delta(x_i), K=400
Alternative CHARMM36 tested; AMBER gave slightly higher CCC on average

subsections.title: Ensemble generation and map composition
content_bullets: Perturb atoms with Gaussian noise matched to per-atom B-factor, locally minimise each conformer with l-BFGS in openMM
Average the blurred maps from ensemble members; grow ensemble size until CCC plateaus
Segmentation: weight each voxel by per-chain responsibility
Composition: combine multiple maps weighted by responsibilities with average k_bg

subsections.title: LoQFit local scoring and benchmark construction
content_bullets: Soft-edged spherical mask centred on Calpha, diameter 5 times global resolution
FSC 0.5 threshold with linear interpolation for smoothness
Benchmark from CERES database, 366 maps at 1.8-7.1 Angstrom, compared against CERES and deposited models

subsections.title: B-factor convergence and robustness
content_bullets: B-factor converges in 10-11 iterations, minimal changes thereafter
Across 366 cases recovered B-factors deviate by 0.87 Angstrom squared across initialisations (B=1, B=1000, U(1,1000), experimental distribution)
Case examples: FBNSV (EMD-10097) and SARS-CoV-2 RdRp (EMD-30127)

subsections.title: Ensemble representation on rotavirus VP6
content_bullets: Rotavirus VP6 (EMD-6272) at 2.6 Angstrom: ensemble map exhibits higher CCC than any single model
SMOCf and RMSFe are anticorrelated along the chain
Mobile side chains (Arg107) yield spread density; rigid side chains (Arg217) yield sharp density

subsections.title: CERES benchmark comparison at 366 maps
content_bullets: Ensemble CCC approaches 0.9 and remains constant across resolution bands while deposited and CERES CCCs decline
Paired t-test: +0.197 vs EMDB (p=0.0006); +0.150 vs CERES (p=0.003)
MolProbity and CaBLAM remain in line with deposited models with wider variability than CERES
Glutamate dehydrogenase (PDB 5K12, EMD-8194, 1.8 Angstrom) illustrates per-residue SMOCf gains; ensemble captures alternative H195 conformers

subsections.title: Segmentation and composition
content_bullets: Herpes Simplex Virus 1 glycoprotein B complex (EMD-21247) segmented into 9 chain submaps without seams
Composition of EMD-12966, EMD-12967, EMD-12968 into a composite; FSC against deposited composite EMD-12969 improves
Responsibility decay yields smooth transitions avoiding double-counted or undercounted boundaries

subsections.title: Case study: yeast RNA polymerase III (PDB 5FJ8, EMD-3178)
content_bullets: Deposited model fit by ChimeraX fitmap: CCC 0.82; TEMPy-REFF single model CCC 0.83; ensemble CCC 0.94
Deposited MolProbity 2.8, clash score 14, Ramachandran outliers 1.1 percent, sidechain outliers 2.1 percent
Average LoQFit chain A: 5.4 Angstrom; chain B: 5.6 Angstrom; chain M: 7.4; chain N: 7.0; chain O: 7.3
Post-refinement averages: chain M 6.0, chain N 5.7, chain O 5.9 Angstrom; global LoQFit improves from 10.1 to 9.0

subsections.title: Case study: nucleosome-CHD4 complex
content_bullets: EMD-10058, PDB 6RYR, resolution range 3-10 Angstrom
Refinement restores DNA base-pair geometry and hydrogen bonding in chains I/J
LoQFit tracks ResMap local resolution

subsections.title: Case study: AlphaFold2 model into SARS-CoV-2 RdRp map
content_bullets: SARS-CoV-2 RdRp at 2.9 Angstrom (EMD-30127, deposited PDB 6M71)
AlphaFold2 prediction from P0DTD1 residues 4393-5324 with templates older than deposition date
Refinement places residues absent from deposited model with high SMOCf; regions of low plDDT correspond to poor SMOCf and low density

sections.title: Discussion
content_bullets: Para 1: joint position/B-factor EM yields a unified probabilistic view of local variability
Para 2: ensemble maps beat single simulated maps for heterogeneous-resolution regions; H195 double conformation is a decisive example
Para 3: ensemble CCC plateaus at moderate ensemble sizes; connection to NMR and X-ray multi-conformer traditions
Para 4: reduced MolProbity/CaBLAM spread relative to Phenix reflects weaker structural restraints; trade-off between outlier suppression and fidelity to data
Para 5: segmentation and composition are naturally resolved by responsibility weighting, avoiding seams; limitations are error propagation from mis-modelled regions
Para 6: AlphaFold2-initialised refinement demonstrates end-to-end automation, opening reproducible, restartable model-building pipelines
